_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C20: strength/power/authority/might/dominion. 370 groups: dimension assignments, dominant_subject assignments, 25 Phase B.5 description rewrites, 5 new Session D pointers, 5 registry stamps, 1 cluster stamp.

set.notes: kreissōn: comparative inner judgment. Moral Character → Cognition.

Humility response to divine might. Agency/Power → Dependence/Creatureliness.

Edification = inner formation. Relational Disposition → Transformation.
[B.5] Inner foundation.

set.context_description: Inner foundation — oikodomeō as figure for the inner-being basis of the person who hears and does God's word: the wise builder on rock (whose inner life does not fail) vs. the foolish builder on sand (whose inner life collapses under trial)

[B.5] Divided or undivided allegiance.

set.context_description: Divided or undivided allegiance — oiketēs as the figure of the household slave whose inner-being orientation is either divided (hating one, loving the other) or wholly aligned (allegiance to one Lord): the characteristic is the inner structure of allegiance and its object (Luk 16:13: no servant can serve two masters)
set.notes: Revised during Dimension Review Phase B.5 (C20, 2026-04-09): term-centric description realigned to characteristic-perspective model. Old: Oiketēs figuring inner-being orientations of allegiance and submission

record.description: The oikos/oikia family in Reg 187 generates groups across at least seven distinct inner-being dimensions (Moral Character, Transformation, Agency/Power, Relational Disposition, Volition, Divine-Human Correspondence, Vitality/Existence). The household vocabulary functions as a structural metaphor for the inner life rather than naming a single characteristic. Session D should assess whether the household/dwelling family constitutes a distinct metaphorical register in the biblical account of the inner person.

The AMTS root family (am.mits, am.tsah, o.mets in Reg 187) is closely cognate with the CHAZAQ family (Reg 33/C08) in the biblical courage formula chazaq ve-amats (be strong and courageous). The split of these two roots across C08 (courage, verb forms) and C20 (strength, adjective/noun forms) is analytically significant. Session D should synthesise the full courage formula by reading the C08 and C20 groups together, addressing the verb/adjective split within the same root complex.

The directionally-determined pattern appears with particular density in C20 strength vocabulary. The same Hebrew root generates groups in opposite registers — divine greatness and human pride (GADAL), strength as divine gift and strength as misplaced trust (OZ, KO.ACH, CHAYIL), sure-footedness and collapse (TSAV). This pattern, established as a structural principle across earlier clusters, is most concentrated in C20. Session D should assess whether the strength/power cluster adds a specific contribution to the directionally-determined principle beyond what C08 (virtue/vice) and C09 (pride/majesty) already established.

The ba.al family (Reg 199) generates a directional pair of particular theological significance: God as ba.al (husband/lord) in the prophetic vision of Israel's restoration (Isa 54:5; Hos 2) vs. ba.al as the false lord toward whom allegiance is misoriented. The same root names both the right and wrong objects of the person's deepest relational orientation (ownership/lordship/marriage). This is structurally parallel to the C07 zeal/jealousy dual-register finding (same passion, sanctified or destructive depending on object). Session D should assess whether the ba.al family extends the zeal/jealousy pattern from inner passion to relational object — and whether there is a broader biblical principle at work: that the terms for the highest forms of inner relational orientation (passion, allegiance, belonging) are precisely the terms most susceptible to catastrophic misorientation.

The basileia (kingdom) family in Reg 199 is the most dimensionally multivalent term in C20, generating groups in Transformation (entry condition), Volition (supreme object of desire), Moral Character (inheritance condition), and Relational Disposition (identity and eschatological hope). The kingdom functions both as the supreme object of inner orientation and as the constitutive condition of the inner being. Session D should assess whether basileia requires a distinctive treatment in the synthesis — a term whose inner-being significance cannot be captured by any single dimension and which may function as an integrating concept for the entire inner-being vocabulary.

operations.description: Registry 187 (strength): Dimension Review complete under v1.7
Registry 196 (power): Dimension Review complete under v1.7
Registry 197 (authority): Dimension Review complete under v1.7
Registry 198 (might): Dimension Review complete under v1.7
Registry 199 (dominion): Dimension Review complete under v1.7

record.notes: C20 complete: strength/power/authority/might/dominion. 370 groups across 5 registries. 25 Phase B.5 rewrites (Reg 187). 5 new Session D pointers. Key findings: dense directionally-determined pattern in strength vocabulary; basileia as most multidimensional term; ba.al family directional pair parallel to C07 zeal pattern; oikos family multi-dimensional metaphorical register; courage formula split (AMTS/CHAZAQ) requires C08+C20 synthesis.
